video:video.video:title: Explosions rock Damascus as French President meets with Syrian counterpart
video:video.video:description: It was the second blast to rock the capital in a few days, and a setback for the country’s new president as he welcomed his first visit from a western leader since ousting longtime dictator Bashar Assad.

video:video.video:title: Why losing weight isn't just about counting calories
video:video.video:description: Here's why the simple math of counting calories doesn’t always add up when it comes to losing weight.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 7
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday July 7th: Democrats pulling endorsements for Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner; Trump heads to NATO summit in Turkey; Tanker hit by projectile, catches fire in Strait of Hormuz; U.S.

video:video.video:title: Joy turns to disappointment for USA World Cup fans from coast to coast
video:video.video:description: USA World Cup soccer fans rode an emotional roller coaster as Monday night's game against Belgium got off to a promising start but ended with a 4-1 loss.

video:video.video:title: Trump says US considering selling F-35 jets to Turkey despite ban
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said the U.S. will lift sanctions on Turkey and consider restoring the sale of F-35 fighter jets to the country, signaling a possible reversal of a ban his administration imposed after Ankara bought Russian missile defense systems.

video:video.video:title: Buckling beams found inside Manhattan building under construction prompting evacuations
video:video.video:description: Reports of falling bricks and buckling columns have prompted evacuations in the area around a Midtown Manhattan high-rise office building that was being converted to luxury apartments, fire officials said Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Reform UK’s Nigel Farage says he’ll quit as a lawmaker and seek re-election to clear name over financial allegations
video:video.video:description: The unexpected resignation is an effort by the anti-immigration politician to preempt a standards investigation into undeclared and potentially rule-breaking donations that could have seen him ejected as a lawmaker, and to present himself as the victim of a witch hunt by the media and his political

video:video.video:title: Falling brick and buckling columns trigger evacuations around Manhattan high-rise
video:video.video:description: The New York Fire Department said on Tuesday that it received reports of bricks falling from the 38-story building at 235 East 42nd Street at around 8 a.m.

video:video.video:title: Judge dismisses Prince Harry's privacy invasion lawsuit against publisher of Daily Mail
video:video.video:description: Prince Harry's final lawsuit aimed at taming the British tabloids ended in defeat Tuesday as a judge said he failed to prove his privacy invasion claims against the publisher of the Daily Mail.

video:video.video:title: Hemingway’s masterpiece on Spain’s bull runs turns 100 years old
video:video.video:description: This year’s San Fermin festival in Spain marked the 100th anniversary of the publication of Ernest Hemingway’s book “The Sun Also Rises” that launched the future Nobel Laureate to literary fame and put Pamplona on the map for millions of people around the world.

video:video.video:title: Trump arrives at NATO summit in Turkey
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump arrived in Turkey on Tuesday to meet with fellow NATO leaders. The summit comes as the alliance tries to persuade him that their countries are actively increasing their military capabilities even as the American focus shifts away from its defense of Europe toward Asia.

video:video.video:title: Truck carrying Khamenei's casket surrounded by crowd of mourners in Qom
video:video.video:description: Mourners dressed in black gathered just south of Qom in Iran for a for late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral service, with throngs of people surrounding the truck carrying his coffin.

video:video.video:title: Police break up Tibet protest outside Chinese Embassy in India
video:video.video:description: Dozens of Tibetan Youth Congress activists protested outside the Chinese Embassy in New Delhi on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: 'The building remains unstable,' New York mayor says about Manhattan building
video:video.video:description: A Manhattan high-rise under construction buckled and bricks tumbled into the street below; Mayor Mamdani says no injuries have been reported.

video:video.video:title: AI stocks resume their drops and drag markets lower worldwide
video:video.video:description: The roller-coaster ride for AI stocks snapped lower again and weighed on Wall Street. The S&P 500 fell 0.4% Tuesday, even though the majority of stocks within the index rose.

video:video.video:title: Tips to talk about money with children
video:video.video:description: Personal finance expert Carrie Joy Grimes shares tips for parents to talk about money with their children

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 7
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, July 7th: New York high-rise buckles, triggers evacuations; explosions injure 18 in Syria during Macron visit; militant attack kills 9 in Pakistan; deadly tropical storm in China forces thousands to evacuate.

video:video.video:title: Manhattan high-rise is still unstable after columns buckle, forcing evacuations
video:video.video:description: An under-construction high-rise in Manhattan was still unstable Tuesday after buckling columns and sagging floors raised fears of a collapse, forcing the tower and other nearby buildings to evacuate.

video:video.video:title: Paraguay senator on racist remarks targeting France captain Kylian Mbappé
video:video.video:description: Paraguay senator Celeste Amarilla on Tuesday said France had no basis to pursue a case against her, days after she posted allegedly racist comments online after soccer star Kylian Mbappé converted the winning penalty in France’s victory over Paraguay in the World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Demonstration in Istanbul against the NATO Summit
video:video.video:description: Thousands of protestors from leftist parties, syndicates and women’s groups on Tuesday marched in central Istanbul against the NATO summit at the end of the first day of the conference.

video:video.video:title: Matt Damon attended rowing camp for 'The Odyssey' preparation
video:video.video:description: 'The Odyssey' star Matt Damon says his role for the film was "not a standard acting job." (July 7)

video:video.video:title: UK and Norwegian PMs shake hands ahead of their countries' World Cup quarter final match
video:video.video:description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer and Norwegian Prime Minister Jonas Gahr Støre posed for photos in their countries' football shirts in Turkey on Tuesday, ahead of the FIFA World Cup quarter final clash between England and Norway on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: What to know as a Manhattan high-rise is unstable after columns buckle, forcing evacuations
video:video.video:description: A Manhattan high-rise remained unstable Tuesday after officials found that columns buckled and floors sagged while it was being converted to luxury apartments, forcing evacuations in and around the Midtown construction site.

video:video.video:title: 'Operation Hard Ball' nets 24 arrests targeting international India-based organized crime gangs
video:video.video:description: Law enforcement officials at federal, local, and international agencies charged 37 alleged members of India-based transnational organized crime groups with multiple criminal acts, including the assassination in Canada of a prominent Indian activist.

video:video.video:title: Democratic states urged to pull Guard troops from Washington
video:video.video:description: The National Guard deployment in Washington, D.C. ballooned during America 250 celebrations, bolstered in part by contingents from Democratic-led states.

video:video.video:title: Hearing for the man accused of killing Charlie Kirk pushes forward
video:video.video:description: The weeklong preliminary hearing for the man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk has entered its second day.

video:video.video:title: Indonesian president meets India's Modi in Jakarta for talks on expanding economic and defense ties
video:video.video:description: The visit marks Modi’s second trip to Indonesia as prime minister, following his previous visit in 2018, and comes after Prabowo attended India’s Republic Day celebrations as chief guest last year, Sugiono, who like many Indonesians uses a single name.

video:video.video:title: Starmer says Farage is calling by-election because he is 'up to his neck in sleaze'
video:video.video:description: "This is a desperate stunt from Nigel Farage, and it's obvious why he's doing it. He is up to his neck in sleaze," UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer said in Ankara, where he is attending a NATO summit.

video:video.video:title: FIFA to investigate racism towards IShowSpeed at World Cup
video:video.video:description: FIFA launches an investigation into racism toward popular streamer IShowSpeed in the stands during the World Cup match between Argentina and Cape Verde.

video:video.video:title: Stars watch fantasy and florals at Chanel
video:video.video:description: Elizabeth Debicki, Anna Wintour and Baz Luhrmann among the guests watching Chanel's Fall-Winter 2026-2027 show, at Paris Haute Couture Week. (July 7)

video:video.video:title: Fantasy and floral romance at Chanel Haute Couture
video:video.video:description: Elizabeth Debicki, Anna Wintour and Baz Luhrmann among the guests watching Chanel's Fall-Winter 2026-2027 show, at Paris Haute Couture Week. (July 7)

video:video.video:title: BTS brings the ARIRANG to London
video:video.video:description: K-Pop superstars played the first of two nights in London, as part of their ARIRANG world tour. (July 7)

video:video.video:title: Egg producers will pay $3.3M and donate 53 million eggs to settle price fixing claims
video:video.video:description: The U.S. Justice Department and 17 states have reached settlement agreements with three major egg producers over allegations that the companies illegally colluded in a multiyear scheme to raises prices.

video:video.video:title: Explosions wound at least 18 in Syria's capital during Macron visit
video:video.video:description: Explosions rocked the Syrian capital Damascus on Tuesday, wounding at least 18 people as French President Emmanuel Macron met with his Syrian counterpart in a landmark visit, local authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Ringo Starr can't quit touring: 'I'm out again'
video:video.video:description: On his 86th birthday, Ringo Starr told The Associated Press that despite repeatedly telling his family each tour would be his last, he keeps returning to the road because music remains central to his life.

video:video.video:title: Bernie Sanders joins Democrats’ calls for Graham Platner to drop out of Maine Senate race
video:video.video:description: A woman who previously dated Maine Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner said he drunkenly forced her to have sex after she told him to stop, leading prominent supporters to pull their endorsements and throwing a must-win race for the party into turmoil.

video:video.video:title: Ringo Starr celebrates 86th birthday
video:video.video:description: Ringo Starr was joined by his wife, Barbara Bach, and friends in Beverly Hills on Tuesday to celebrate his 86th birthday.

video:video.video:title: US launches new strikes on Iran, revokes oil sales permit after 3 ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military attacked Iran early Wednesday after it said Tehran struck three ships in the Strait of Hormuz, part of an American effort that also revoked the Islamic Republic's ability to openly sell crude oil in the world market.

video:video.video:title: Chinese men in Germany used Telegram to share rape videos and drugging tips
video:video.video:description: A German investigation into an online predator network that thrived on Telegram for years has led to the convictions of three alleged members of the group's inner circle and the ongoing trial of a fourth in Berlin.

video:video.video:title: Trump insists again that US should control Greenland
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said the semiautonomous island, which is part of NATO ally Denmark, is “an important part for the United States,” and that he does not intend to let Greenland be threatened by China and Russia.

video:video.video:title: Sirens sound in Kuwait after it says its air defences intercepted incoming fire
video:video.video:description: Sirens sounded in Kuwait on Wednesday as the country's air defenses engaged to intercept incoming fire after the United States launched airstrikes targeting Iran.

video:video.video:title: Funeral for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei at Najaf airport in Iraq
video:video.video:description: An official funeral ceremony was held Tuesday night for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei at Najaf International airport in Iraq.

video:video.video:title: US military attacks Iran after it said Tehran struck three ships in the Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military has attacked Iran after it said Tehran struck three ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Landslides in Bangladesh kill at least eight Rohingya refugees
video:video.video:description: Landslides triggered by heavy monsoon rains in southeastern Bangladesh killed at least eight Rohingya refugees, including five children, in Cox’s Bazar.

video:video.video:title: Thousands participate in funeral procession for late Iranian Supreme Leader Khamenei in Iraq
video:video.video:description: Thousands of people walked in the funeral ceremonies for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in the Iraqi city of Najaf Wednesday morning.

video:video.video:title: Compilation video shows Tyler Robinson on the day of Charlie Kirk's killing, investigator testifies
video:video.video:description: Tyler Robinson, the man charged with killing Charlie Kirk, strolled Utah Valley University in shorts and a T-shirt and bought a meal at Chick-fil-A before returning in different clothes to shoot the conservative activist from a rooftop, an investigator testified Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: UN warns of global 'catastrophic consequences' amid renewed military confrontations in the Gulf
video:video.video:description: Spokesperson Stéphane Dujarric made clear in a briefing at the United Nations Wednesday that the Secretary-General is "alarmed" by renewed military attacks in the Gulf, adding that they, " risk derailing the diplomatic progress achieved between the Islamic Republic of Iran and the United States."

video:video.video:title: Tourists and New Yorkers face uncertainty after Manhattan high-rise scare
video:video.video:description: People evacuated from buildings near a Manhattan high-rise with buckled columns describe spending the night without a place to stay. (AP Video: Ted Shaffrey)

video:video.video:title: Trump says US will give Ukraine license to make Patriot missiles to counter Russia
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said the US will give a license to Ukraine to build Patriot air defense systems, a major boost for Kyiv as it battles Russia.

video:video.video:title: Hospitalized Mitch McConnell confers with GOP leaders amid health speculation
video:video.video:description: The Senate's top two Republicans have spoken individually to Kentucky Sen. Mitch McConnell, according to aides, as the former GOP leader remains in the hospital more than three weeks after being admitted for undisclosed health issues.

video:video.video:title: Stocks fall, oil soars after Trump says ceasefire with Iran is 'over'
video:video.video:description: Oil prices rose, and stock markets dropped in shaky trading worldwide Wednesday after President Donald Trump raised doubts about the temporary truce in the war with Iran.

video:video.video:title: Iran military releases footage it says shows missile launches against US forces
video:video.video:description: Iran's military released footage said to show missiles being launched in response to what it described as "U.S. aggressions and breach of commitments."

video:video.video:title: Iran for the first time releases video of Khamenei residence after attacks
video:video.video:description: The official website of Iran's slain supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and the country's state TV on Wednesday aired video purportedly showing a mosque in his residence compound after U.S-Israeli attacks in the war.

video:video.video:title: Trump wants a new White House helipad. Here’s why
video:video.video:description: The White House is getting a new helipad. President Donald Trump says the upgraded landing pad is needed for newer, more powerful presidential helicopters.

video:video.video:title: Venezuela earthquake survivors face water shortages, receive aid packages
video:video.video:description: Venezuelans affected by the devastating earthquakes last month received some aid on Wednesday but increasingly face challenges accessing clean water as well as sanitation and hygiene services.

video:video.video:title: Several explosions rock Iran as US launches new strikes
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump on Wednesday warned Iran that the U.S. was preparing for another night of strikes, just hours after he said the ceasefire was over but negotiations can continue.

video:video.video:title: Trump declaring ceasefire 'over' with Iran overshadows NATO Summit
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump threatened Wednesday to unleash more military strikes against Iran during a two-day NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey, where he said Iranian attacks signaled the end of the ceasefire.

video:video.video:title: Son demands independent probe after father shot and killed by ICE officer in Houston
video:video.video:description: The son of a Houston man killed by an Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer is demanding an independent investigation after Federal agents say he attempted to ram an agent with his van.

video:video.video:title: Families await news of 5 missing crew as cargo plane wreckage found off Pakistan's coast
video:video.video:description: The family of co-pilot Faisal Jatoi, one of the crew members of a cargo plane crashed in Pakistan’s Arabian sea, were awaiting his safe recovery on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Trump says interim agreement between U.S. and Iran is 'over'
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump made the comments on the sidelines of the two-day NATO summit in Ankara, Turkey following U.S. strikes on Iran in reaction to attacks on three ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Rome prepares for heatwaves by helping elderly people who live alone
video:video.video:description: The project, called “Viva gli anziani” (“Long Live the Elderly”), aims to assist older people not only by bringing them groceries and providing financial help, but also by building a social community around them.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 8
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday July 8th: Trump says he thinks Iran interim agreement is over; Denmark’s Prime Minister says Denmark is ready to defend Greenland; Funeral procession in Iraq for late Iranian Supreme Leader; New York City building stabilized after collapse risk.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 8
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, July 8th: Trump warns Iran, U.S. preparing more strikes; Trump allows Patriot missiles made for Ukraine; Gov.

video:video.video:title: Sam Ryder: theater superstar
video:video.video:description: Sam Ryder is feeling the love at the opening night of his theatrical debut, as "Jesus Christ Superstar" at the London Palladium.

video:video.video:title: NATO summit opens in Ankara, Turkey
video:video.video:description: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte and Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan, who is hosting the summit, both spoke at the opening of the event.

video:video.video:title: Judge dismisses Prince Harry's privacy invasion lawsuit against publisher of Daily Mail
video:video.video:description: Prince Harry’s final lawsuit aimed at taming the British tabloids ended in defeat Tuesday as a judge said he failed to prove his privacy invasion claims against the publisher of the Daily Mail.

video:video.video:title: Cardi B models Robert Wun's 'Childsplay' couture
video:video.video:description: Hong Kong-born, London-based designer Robert Wun unveiled his "Childsplay" haute couture collection in Paris Wednesday, with Cardi B and Fan Bingbing sitting front row.

video:video.video:title: Kentucky farmer adapts harvest, protects crops during extreme heat
video:video.video:description: The recent heat dome affected some specialty farmers who produce crops like fruits and vegetables. Intense heat is the result of a human-driven climate change pattern of weather extremes that may lead to loss of crops for farmers and forces them to adapt their practices.

video:video.video:title: We asked children who taught them about money
video:video.video:description: We asked children who taught them about money

video:video.video:title: Landslides in Bangladesh kill at least 8 Rohingya refugees
video:video.video:description: Landslides triggered by heavy monsoon rains in southeastern Bangladesh have killed at least eight Rohingya refugees, including five children, while heavy monsoon rains battered parts of neighboring India, leaving 13 dead over the past few days.

video:video.video:title: Trump threatens to strike Iran's civilian infrastructure: 'They really deserve it'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump threatened Wednesday to strike Iran's civilian infrastructure and suggested the U.S. could seize Kharg Island, home to critical oil facilities, as a fragile ceasefire appeared to unravel.

video:video.video:title: Denmark hits back after Trump suggests once again that U.S. should control Greenland
video:video.video:description: U.S President Donald Trump reopened old wounds over Greenland on the eve of the meeting by insisting that the United States should control the semiautonomous island.

video:video.video:title: Iran issues warning after US launches strikes and limits sale of the country's oil
video:video.video:description: Iranian State television aired a statement on Wednesday from Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters, Iran's joint military command, warning countries in the region that "providing support" to the U.S. will make them "a legitimate target".

video:video.video:title: French Far-right leader Marine Le Pen plans to run for president with court-ordered ankle tag
video:video.video:description: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen was sentenced Tuesday to wear a court-ordered electronic monitor after a conviction for embezzlement.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians in Gaza watch World Cup clash between Egypt and Argentina
video:video.video:description: Egypt shares Gaza’s only Arab border through the Rafah crossing and has long served as one of the main mediators in ceasefire negotiations between Israel and Hamas, alongside Qatar, Turkey and the United States.

video:video.video:title: Trump casts doubt on Iran deal, says any fighting will end 'very quickly'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump closed the NATO summit Wednesday by warning of more strikes on Iran just hours after he said the ceasefire was over, but he claimed any fighting will be over quickly.

video:video.video:title: Trump hails 'tremendous love,' unity at NATO summit
video:video.video:description: A day after blasting allies for failing to support the U.S. in its war with Iran, President Donald Trump hailed the NATO summit in Turkey as "very successful" and filled with "tremendous love," praising the alliance's unity as he departed the two-day event.

video:video.video:title: China animal shelter owner takes great risks to evacuate dogs and cats from flooding
video:video.video:description: Mo Xingjian who runs a small animal shelter in the city of Nanning, wades through growing flood water from his home to rescue cats and dogs under his care.

video:video.video:title: Police inspect Waymo self-driving car after detaining two teenagers
video:video.video:description: Police in Northern California detained two 15-year-olds after Waymo alerted them to the teens' activities.

video:video.video:title: Trump describes Iran as weakened but alludes to ongoing security concerns
video:video.video:description: "I'm their number one target." Trump told reporters on Wednesday before answering questions about security concerns with flying home aboard newly gifted Air Force One plane.

video:video.video:title: Mixed reactions at Freedom Fuel gas station in Philadelphia suburbs
video:video.video:description: The station in Dresher, Pennsylvania, is the first Freedom Fuel gas station in the network to open in the region, according to the White House’s post on the social media platform X.

video:video.video:title: Can AI change lives for the better?
video:video.video:description: AI has been dominating news headlines in recent years as people digest what the developments of large language models will mean for the future.

video:video.video:title: Thousands in funeral procession for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei in Karbala
video:video.video:description: Funeral processions for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei began Wednesday in the Iraqi cities of Najaf and Karbala with thousands of mourners present.

video:video.video:title: Sirens sound in Kuwait and other Gulf countries as tensions between US and Iran increase
video:video.video:description: Sirens sounded in Kuwait and alerts were activated in Qatar and Bahrain due to retaliatory Iranian fire over the Gulf countries after the United States launched new airstrikes hitting Iran early Thursday.

video:video.video:title: How far fans will go for World Cup glory
video:video.video:description: How far would you go to see your team win the World Cup? We asked fans what they'd be willing to give up for soccer's biggest prize.

video:video.video:title: US says it has ended strikes on Iran after hitting some 90 targets
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said Thursday it had ended its latest round of airstrikes targeting Iran after hitting some 90 targets.

video:video.video:title: Indian Prime Minister Modi greeted by Australian counterpart ahead of talks in Melbourne
video:video.video:description: Australia's Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and his Indian counterpart Narendra Modi attended the Australia-India Economic Roadmap Business Reception in Melbourne on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Maine Democrat Graham Platner in a social media post says he plans to withdraw from Senate race
video:video.video:description: Democrat Graham Platner says he plans to withdraw from the U.S. Senate race in Maine after a woman who dated him said he drunkenly forced her to have sex despite her telling him to stop.

video:video.video:title: Maine Democrat Graham Platner drops US Senate bid after woman's sexual assault allegation
video:video.video:description: Democrat Graham Platner has dropped out of the U.S. Senate race in Maine after a woman who dated him said he drunkenly forced her to have sex despite her telling him to stop.

video:video.video:title: Mourners cry and push to get close to coffin of Iran’s late Supreme Leader in Karbala
video:video.video:description: Thousands of mourners cried, pushed each other and shoved their way to get close the coffin of Iran’s late Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, at the Imam Hussein shrine in Karbala.

video:video.video:title: Abidjan hosts 3-day African Space Solutions Market to put satellites and drones to work
video:video.video:description: Abidjan, the economic capital of Ivory Coast, hosts the second edition of an African space technology trade show known as the African Space Solutions Market.

video:video.video:title: US launches new airstrikes on Iran, as Tehran fires back at Gulf Arab states
video:video.video:description: The United States launched new airstrikes against Iran early Thursday, and Tehran responded by targeting Bahrain, Kuwait and Qatar in crossfire that again threatened an interim deal intended to help end the war.

video:video.video:title: English court to rule on final challenge to Trinidad's gay sex ban
video:video.video:description: A nearly 10-year battle for gay rights in Trinidad and Tobago could end soon at a final appeals court in England.

video:video.video:title: Chloe Sevigny on upcoming Grateful Dead documentary 'Summer Tour'
video:video.video:description: While at "The Five-Star Weekend" premiere, longtime Grateful Dead fan Chloe Sevigny opened up about the Grateful Dead documentary she executive produced.

video:video.video:title: Jennifer Garner and the cast of 'Five-Star Weekend' pose at the Los Angeles premiere
video:video.video:description: Jennifer Garner, Gemma Chan, Chloe Sevigny and D'Arcy Carden attend the Los Angeles premiere of Peacock's "The Five-Star Weekend." (July 8)

video:video.video:title: Morocco seeks to avenge 2022 loss to France at World Cup semifinals, France expects strong challenge
video:video.video:description: Morocco is set to meet France in the first cup of the 2026 World Cup quarterfinals. It is a rematch of the 2022 World Cup semifinal in Qatar, where France ended Morocco’s historic run with a 2-0 victory.

video:video.video:title: Jennifer Garner says she's quick to choose projects that bring production back to LA
video:video.video:description: Jennifer Garner only has two criteria for her projects: She wants to stay close to home in Los Angeles to be with family and make sure to keep local crew members employed throughout the city.

video:video.video:title: Meet Count Binface: The comedian who wants to challenge UK's Nigel Farage
video:video.video:description: Facing awkward questions about his finances, the anti-immigration politician dramatically announced that he’s quitting Parliament in order to run again, a move critics denounced as a ploy to dodge a parliamentary probe.

video:video.video:title: Emmy nominees describe how they reacted to their happy news
video:video.video:description: 2026 Emmy nominees including "The Pitt" actors Katherine LaNasa and Tal Anderson, and “The Daily Show” host Jordan Klepper, reveal how they felt when they heard the news.
